stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Neuroscientists Mira Bansal and Tomás Reyes sought to better understand how the auditory cortex processes complex sounds.
  • They built a study using computational models of cortical activity.
  • They found that specific cortical fibers let the brain switch between two listening modes.
  • In one mode, cells in the auditory cortex capture a snapshot of the sound at a given moment.
  • In the other mode, those cells track changes in the sound across time.
stem: The student wants to summarize the study's findings. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: To arrive at these findings, which describe two listening modes in the auditory cortex, Bansal and Reyes built a study using computational models.
According to Bansal and Reyes, the brain can switch between capturing a snapshot of a sound at a moment and tracking how the sound changes over time.
Using computational models, Bansal and Reyes built a study to better understand how the auditory cortex processes complex sounds.
Bansal and Reyes showed that sounds are captured by cells in the auditory cortex, where the perception of sound forms.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer because it states the actual findings of the study: that the brain toggles between snapshot and tracking modes.

Choice A focuses on methodology rather than findings. Choice C describes the study's purpose. Choice D states an unremarkable general fact rather than a finding.
